description_long: This object consists of two distinct stone structures placed side by side. The left structure is a tall, slender obelisk with a square base, a cylindrical shaft featuring subtle horizontal bands, and a sculpted figure at the top. The right structure is a statue depicting a robed figure, possibly a woman, holding a child in front of her, both rendered in a classical style. The surfaces of both elements are weathered, with visible discoloration and rough stone texture, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The obelisk's base is simple and blocky, while the statue's base is polygonal and slightly elevated. The overall appearance is reminiscent of a memorial or monument commonly found in parks, plazas, or near historical sites.
description: A weathered stone obelisk with a sculpted figure at the top stands beside a classical statue of a robed figure holding a child, both on sturdy bases.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height of the obelisk with its sculpted top and the detailed faces of the robed figure and child statue.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the profile of the robed figure and child, as well as the cylindrical shaft of the obelisk.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the robed figure and the plain back of the obelisk, with less visible detail.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the alignment of the obelisk and statue, with the obelisk partially obscuring the statue.
attribute_ornamentation: The obelisk features a sculpted figure at its tip, and the adjacent statue is ornately carved to depict a robed figure holding a child. Both elements have detailed facial and garment features.
attribute_base_design: The obelisk stands on a square, blocky stone base with a slightly raised platform, while the statue has a polygonal base that is simple and unadorned.
attribute_tip_design: The tip of the obelisk is adorned with a sculpted figure, possibly a person or angel, adding a decorative and symbolic element.
